mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, reflecting a healthy economic backdrop.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when RSAFS shows positive year-over-year growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation above a 4% annualized rate signals rising input costs and potential central bank tightening, which erodes corporate margins and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 4%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low, indicating a tight labor market, and the Federal Funds Rate is actively rising, it signals an overheating economy prompting aggressive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts corporate valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 5% and FEDFUNDS has risen by more than 0.25% over 3 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing and supply-side activity, signaling a broader economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns, especially during periods of economic contraction.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where INDPRO 3-month average growth is negative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts signals a weakening housing market and broader economic demand, which can lead to reduced consumer spending and investment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods following a 10% year-over-year decline in HOUST over 3 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market and strong consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when PAYEMS 3-month average growth exceeds 0.1% month-over-month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank, which increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a single month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong annualized GDP growth reflects a healthy and expanding economy, indicating robust corporate profits and investor optimism, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.
